examples.title: Bully Algorithm - Election Initiation
examples.description: Process initiates election when it detects coordinator failure
examples.code:
PROCEDURE initiate_election()
 current_process = self.id
 FOR each process with id > current_process
 SEND election_message TO process
 END FOR
 
 wait_time = TIMEOUT
 received_ok = FALSE
 
 WHILE wait_time > 0
 IF received OK message FROM any higher_id_process THEN
 // Higher process will handle election
 RETURN
 END IF
 wait_time = wait_time - 1
 END WHILE
 
 // No response - become coordinator
 become_coordinator()
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: When a process detects coordinator failure, it sends election messages to all processes with higher IDs. If no response is received within timeout, the process becomes the new coordinator.

examples.title: Ring Algorithm - Token Passing Election
examples.description: Election using logical ring with token passing
examples.code:
PROCEDURE start_ring_election()
 election_list = [self.id]
 current = self.next_process
 
 // Collect all IDs in the ring
 WHILE current != self
 IF current.is_active THEN
 election_list.append(current.id)
 END IF
 current = current.next
 END WHILE
 
 // Find maximum ID
 max_id = MAX(election_list)
 leader = PROCESS_WITH_ID(max_id)
 
 // Announce leader
 BROADCAST coordinator_message(leader)
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: The ring algorithm collects process IDs as a token circulates through the ring. Once the token returns to the initiator, the highest ID is identified as the new leader and announced to all processes.

examples.title: Coordinator Announcement
examples.description: Broadcasting the newly elected coordinator to all processes
examples.code:
PROCEDURE become_coordinator()
 FOR each process in system
 SEND coordinator_message(self.id) TO process
 END FOR
 
 // Start sending heartbeats
 START heartbeat_timer()
 WHILE system_running
 BROADCAST heartbeat(self.id)
 WAIT heartbeat_interval
 END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: After being elected, the new coordinator announces its leadership to all processes and begins sending periodic heartbeats to maintain its authority and allow failure detection.

examples.title: Failure Detection and Election Trigger
examples.description: Monitoring for coordinator failure and triggering election
examples.code:
PROCEDURE monitor_coordinator()
 last_heartbeat = current_time()
 
 WHILE system_running
 IF received heartbeat FROM coordinator THEN
 last_heartbeat = current_time()
 END IF
 
 IF (current_time() - last_heartbeat) > timeout THEN
 // Coordinator failed
 LOG("Coordinator failure detected")
 initiate_election()
 RETURN
 END IF
 
 WAIT monitoring_interval
 END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Each process monitors the coordinator by tracking heartbeats. If no heartbeat is received within the timeout period, the process detects failure and initiates a new election.
